methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Waabi, a Series C autonomous vehicle software company founded by Raquel Urtasun, operates within a Transportation sector peer set comprising seven private companies and zero public comparables. The narrow peer universe—including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft—lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage density to support a reliable peer-median valuation method.

Stage-progression analysis, applied to Waabi's $1.0B in total raised capital, yields the primary valuation signal. Using a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C progression, this method implies a $1.7B valuation. The 1.7x multiplier reflects observed advancement from Series C to subsequent funding rounds in comparable-stage companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B to $2.0B, with a median point of $1.7B. This range incorporates typical variance around the stage-progression estimate but is constrained by reliance on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.7x multiplier for autonomous vehicle software specifically, given the narrow peer set; the absence of public market comparables; and whether total capital raised is an appropriate proxy for current enterprise value absent near-term exit signals or updated financials. Additional data on revenue, burn rate, or recent fundraising terms would materially strengthen confidence in this range.
